title: Universal fusion category symmetries on tensor products of infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces
abstract: We show that anyon chains, after stabilizing with infinite-dimensional ancilla spaces, factorize locally as tensor products of infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces. This implies that any unitary fusion category can be realized as symmetries on a tensor product of infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces. We then show that any two anyon chains with the same symmetry category are related by a symmetry-compatible locality-preserving unitary after stabilizing with infinite-dimensional ancilla, showing that for a fixed fusion category, there is a single stable equivalence class of symmetry realizations on the lattice via anyon chains. As a corollary of our proof, we show that the physical boundary algebras of Levin-Wen type models are bounded spread isomorphic after stabilization if and only if they have the same bulk topological order.
summary: This paper provides a universal classification for the kinematic realization of fusion category symmetries on 1D lattices. By introducing a stabilization process using infinite-dimensional ancilla, the authors show that all anyon chains realizing the same symmetry category are equivalent, effectively reducing a complex 'zoo' of models to a single stable equivalence class.
main_problem: The paper aims to classify the different ways a fixed fusion category can be realized as a symmetry on a lattice (kinematical realization) and to resolve the 'zoo' of anyon chain variations using a physically motivated stable equivalence relation.
main_result: The authors prove that after stabilizing with infinite-dimensional ancilla, any anyon chain admits a local decomposition into a tensor product of infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, and for a fixed fusion category, there is a single stable equivalence class of symmetry realizations.
method: The study utilizes the mathematical framework of quasi-local C*-algebras, W*-algebras, DHR theory, and the construction of W*-tensor categories via infinite-dimensional Hilbert space enrichment.
model_or_system: The models studied are anyon chains and fusion spin chains (boundary algebras of Levin-Wen type string-net models) defined on 1D lattices, specifically utilizing infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces and ancilla to achieve stabilization.
key_observables: Symmetry category, Drinfeld center, symmetry order algebra, and bounded spread isomorphism class.
important_parameters: The stabilization process via infinite-dimensional ancilla, the localization length R, and the strong tensor generator X.
main_assumptions: The results assume the use of infinite-dimensional ancilla for stabilization and rely on the properties of unitary fusion/multi-fusion categories and local normality of the algebras.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the mathematical requirements for symmetry (Haag duality, local normality) to constructing the 'standard model' of anyon chains, then moves to proving the universality of these realizations via stabilization, and concludes with the classification of fusion spin chains based on their bulk topological order.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
